FUTUREPROOF
FOR STUDENT + COUNSELOR USE ONLY
FutureProof · Indiana University-Bloomington · Marketing/Marketing Management, General
Page 2
Sources: BLS OOH · College Scorecard · O*NET · Ka
rpathy AI Exposure · BEA RPP. Powered by Gemma 4.
ABOUT THIS CAREER
Fundraisers secure the money needed to run organizations and support their goals. You build long-term relationships with
donors and communicate with people outside your group to gather support. The work involves gathering information and
creating strategies to meet financial objectives.
Day-to-day
•  Build and maintain relationships with donors
 
•  Communicate with people outside the organization
 
•  Gather information to support goals
 
•  Develop objectives and strategies
 
•  Make decisions to solve problems
 
SUGGESTED SKILLS
Bring this list to your admissions counselor — these are the skills the data says will lift your outcomes.
CAREER-LAUNCH SKILLS
• Public Relations Club
ERN+1
 Coursework
 Clubs / orgs
 Internship / cert
Ask: "Building a network of peers helps you practice the interpersonal skills needed to connect with donors."
• Nonprofit Management Minor
ROI+2
 Coursework
 Clubs / orgs
 Internship / cert
Ask: "Understanding how organizations handle money makes you a more effective fundraiser."
• Consumer Behavior Course
RES+1
 Coursework
 Clubs / orgs
 Internship / cert
Ask: "Studying why people make choices helps you solve problems when trying to influence supporters."
• Creative Writing Course
GRW+1
 Coursework
 Clubs / orgs
 Internship / cert
Ask: "Learning to tell stories helps you communicate a cause in a way that moves people to give."
QUESTIONS & FOLLOW-UPS
ASK THE COLLEGE
• Which majors at Indiana University-Bloomington most often lead graduates into Fundraisers?
• How can I augment this major with the suggested skills above — through coursework, clubs, or internships you already offer?
• What outcomes data do you publish for this program — median earnings one year out, employment rate, average debt at
graduation?
ASK YOUR PARENTS
• If the loan numbers on page 1 are accurate, can our family carry that monthly payment alongside everything else after I graduate?
• Whose career did you watch up close growing up — and what about it would you want for me, or want to spare me from?
ASK YOURSELF
• Will I still want to be doing this work in 10 years if the day-to-day looks like the O*NET task profile on page 1?
• Am I picking this major because it interests me, or because it's familiar — and would I know the difference yet?

CIP

Programs) — the standard for naming college majors.
RES
AI Resilience — how much of this occupation's work is
hard for AI to do, blended from task-level data.

FutureProof · Harvard University · Computer Science.
Page 2
Sources: BLS OOH · College Scorecard · O*NET · Ka
rpathy AI Exposure · BEA RPP. Powered by Gemma 4.
ABOUT THIS CAREER
Keep computer networks running and secure for organizations. You solve technical problems and manage the hardware and
software that connects people. This role involves constant learning to stay current with new technology.
Day-to-day
•  Work directly with computer systems
 
•  Research information to solve technical issues
 
•  Update technical knowledge regularly
 
•  Communicate with supervisors and teammates
 
•  Build professional relationships with colleagues
 
SUGGESTED SKILLS
Bring this list to your admissions counselor — these are the skills the data says will lift your outcomes.
QUESTIONS & FOLLOW-UPS
ASK THE COLLEGE
• Which majors at Harvard University most often lead graduates into Computer network support specialists?
• How can I augment this major with the suggested skills above — through coursework, clubs, or internships you already offer?
• Show me how the Computer Science department integrates curriculum to mitigate AI displacement risks for network specialists.
• Publish the specific career placement data for Harvard Computer Science graduates entering technical support roles.
ASK YOUR PARENTS
• Can our family cover the high debt burden associated with this Harvard tuition without compromising our long-term stability?
• How much of this debt service can you spare if the job market outlook for network specialists remains only moderate?
ASK YOURSELF
• Will I tolerate the moderate burnout risk inherent in a career as a computer network support specialist?
• Am I prepared to pivot my skills if AI displacement impacts this specific career path?

FutureProof · Miami Dade College · Registered Nursing, Nursing Administration, Nursing Research and Clinical Nursing.
Page 2
Sources: BLS OOH · College Scorecard · O*NET · Ka
rpathy AI Exposure · BEA RPP. Powered by Gemma 4.
ABOUT THIS CAREER
Registered nurses provide direct care and support to patients in various healthcare settings. They use medical knowledge to
monitor health, solve clinical problems, and manage patient information. The work focuses on maintaining patient safety and
coordinating care through constant observation and documentation.
Day-to-day
•  Provide direct care and assistance to patients
 
•  Apply medical knowledge to patient situations
 
•  Solve problems and make clinical decisions
 
•  Record and document patient information
 
•  Gather health information from patients and staff
 
SUGGESTED SKILLS
Bring this list to your admissions counselor — these are the skills the data says will lift your outcomes.
QUESTIONS & FOLLOW-UPS
ASK THE COLLEGE
• Which majors at Miami Dade College most often lead graduates into Registered nurses?
• How can I augment this major with the suggested skills above — through coursework, clubs, or internships you already offer?
• Show me the specific clinical placement rates for Miami Dade College nursing students.
• Connect me with alumni who have transitioned from clinical nursing into nursing administration.
ASK YOUR PARENTS
• Can our family cover the remaining costs if the debt burden exceeds the low estimate shown here?
• How much of this tuition can you spare if I decide to pursue nursing research instead of clinical work?
ASK YOURSELF
• Will I sustain my performance if I hit the moderate burnout risk levels common in nursing?
• Do I have the stamina to reach the earnings ceiling indicated for this career path?

ABOUT THIS CAREER
Design and develop aircraft, spacecraft, and satellites. Use computer software to analyze complex data and solve technical
problems. Research information to make informed decisions about flight systems and structures.
Day-to-day
•  Use computers to design and model systems
 
•  Analyze technical data and information
 
•  Process complex engineering information
 
•  Solve technical problems and make decisions
 
•  Research and gather new information
 
SUGGESTED SKILLS
Bring this list to your admissions counselor — these are the skills the data says will lift your outcomes.
QUESTIONS & FOLLOW-UPS
ASK THE COLLEGE
• Which majors at California Institute of Technology most often lead graduates into Aerospace engineers?
• How can I augment this major with the suggested skills above — through coursework, clubs, or internships you already offer?
• Show me how the Mechanical Engineering department integrates machine learning to mitigate AI displacement risks in aerospace
design.
• Connect me with alumni who have navigated the elevated earnings ceiling in the aerospace sector.
ASK YOUR PARENTS
• Can our family cover the cost of this program if the debt-to-earnings ratio remains high due to the projected earnings ceiling?
• How much of our savings should we set aside to spare me from high debt service if the aerospace job market remains low?
ASK YOURSELF
• Will I sustain the daily workload of an aerospace engineer if the burnout risk is moderate?
• Do I have the technical aptitude to stay ahead of the automation occurring in mechanical engineering?

GEMMA'S VERDICT
Miami Dade College focuses on getting you into the workforce quickly with very little debt. You optimize for financial safety
because your starting salary for Registered nurses covers your $25,070 in student loans in about one year. The tradeoff is that
the school name carries less weight on a resume and you might hit a career ceiling sooner than in other fields. California
Institute of Technology optimizes for high prestige and a high median salary of $134,830 as an Aerospace engineer. This path
offers a growing job market but requires you to take on a large amount of debt, totaling $167,196. You are trading financial
ease and protection against automation for a powerful brand name and a high-level technical career. Harvard University
optimizes for extreme brand recognition and a very high starting salary of $140,072 for Computer network support
specialists. However, the cost of the degree is very high, leaving you with $165,684 in debt that takes longer to pay back
relative to your earnings. You also face a higher risk of your specific job tasks being changed by AI technology. Your decision
comes down to whether you want to prioritize immediate financial stability or invest heavily in a high-prestige name for
long-term career potential.
Big Choice
Low Debt or High Prestige
Nursing at Miami Dade College costs only $25,070 in debt compared to $167,196 for Caltech or $165,684 for Harvard.
